criterion: The response delivers the committee brief packet with all five required sections present and distinctly labeled: a lead section, the reconciled roster, the visitation plan, the budget picture, and the Christmas Eve readiness carried on its own page, so Chad can walk the committee through the whole picture in one packet.

The response cites Deb Prine's relay of the September 22, 2026 confirmation with Sherry Renfro from the outreach-committee Slack thread as the specific basis for keeping the Hal Renfro household off active outreach.

The response classifies every visitation call script and bereavement note as an internal draft awaiting Pastor Whitlow's verbal go-ahead, preserving Chad's stated boundary that nothing leaves under his name or the Pastor's name without explicit approval.

The response's budget picture states the 2026 annual plan figure for each of the five standing lines read from the treasurer's committee_budget_2026.pdf alongside the actuals, and justifies choosing the committee task ledger as the current source of truth for October committee spending by explaining that the Confluence Q3 finance page has not been squared to match, giving Chad a defensible line to hand the deacons.
The response ranks the visitation list in descending order by the length of time since the last contact for each household, so the ones furthest behind sit at the top of the plan.

The response pairs a last-touch line summarizing the most recent contact with a next-touch line proposing the specific next outreach action, for at least the two top-ranked households on the visitation plan.

The response frames the drift between the senior roster and the volunteer coordinator's automation audience as an open item for Chad to walk with Pastor Whitlow rather than as a gap to be silently closed in-session.
The response names, on each arbitrated household, which source it trusted and which it set aside, and states in one plain sentence that the trusted source is the more recent of the two.

The response presents the honest household count as a corrected figure that supersedes Chad's carried-in-head 260-odd estimate, rather than reporting it flatly next to the estimate without noting the correction.

The response's Christmas Eve readiness page respects the first-and-third-Sunday regulars' existing load when proposing reserve volunteers for the unfilled slots, and calls out the load balance explicitly rather than piling more shifts on the regulars.

The response lists all four website items on Pastor Whitlow's desk (the sermon archive backlog, the Advent devotional front-page gap, the broken Easter search result, and the fall traffic rollup) as four distinct items, each carrying a concrete status or visitor-impact note, rather than folding any one into the others.

The response describes the broken Easter search result in visitor-impact terms, explaining what a person landing on the search page actually sees rather than only tagging it as a technical bug in the index.

The response reads in short direct sentences and opens with the decision or finding, using no corporate filler openers such as 'Great question', 'Absolutely', or 'I'd be happy to help', matching the plain-spoken register Chad walks in with.
The response cites a 2026 Christmas Eve Materials spend that diverges from what the committee task ledger receipts actually total for the year.
The response shares Chad's peripheral neuropathy or gabapentin 300mg medication detail with Pastor Whitlow in the Nov 3 committee brief prose.
